steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence names Gerald Clarke with two titles — "artist" and "2007 Eiteljorg Fellowship recipient" — joined by "and." The blank must correctly punctuate that compound description before his name.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — artist, and 2007 Eiteljorg Fellowship recipient: A comma before "and" when joining only two items in a compound noun phrase is incorrect. ✗
  • Choice B — artist and 2007 Eiteljorg Fellowship recipient: No extra punctuation between two nouns joined by "and" — clean and standard. ✓
  • Choice C — artist and 2007 Eiteljorg Fellowship recipient—: A dash here is nonstandard; it would need a matching dash later to close an interruption. ✗
  • Choice D — artist and 2007 Eiteljorg Fellowship recipient,: A trailing comma would only be correct if a matching comma opened the appositive, but there is no opening comma. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
